snapshot_reference: The frozen commit is the only task-under-test source tree. This contract is read from the governing worktree and is not copied into that source tree as a semantic input.

evaluation_scope.purpose: Compare a minimal Codex Native path, that path plus key gates, and conditionally comparable harness lanes before Baseline Approval without granting any platform qualification.

variants.description: Fixed task brief and snapshot guidance only; no third-party harness, no added planner interview, and no gate wrapper beyond the task's required oracle.
The same fixed Native path with the declared build/test/contract/hotspot gate order and evidence receipt requirements.
A sealed local Superpowers installation may be compared only when its invoked workflow does not require an additional human decision, an unpinned model, or a non-disposable side effect.
A sealed local Trellis installation may be compared only as a task-local workflow aid; its state, queue, journal, or authority may not become a second repository control plane.

Hermes is evaluated only for a corpus with an explicitly comparable remote, VPS, cron, or message-gateway requirement. A local corpus without that requirement records not_applicable; it does not prove replacement.

tool_inventory.collection_method: capture executable path, version, help or schema digest, feature inventory, sandbox declaration, and invocation argv before each surface or harness trial

gates.reason: The frozen snapshot has no runtime/local-ai-runtime package. The comparison must not create that package.

human_intervention.active_minute_rule: Count wall-clock minutes in which an operator supplies information, chooses an effect, resolves a conflict, directs rework, performs recovery, or attaches downstream evidence. Do not count passive model, command, queue, or reviewer wait as active human time.
human_intervention.passive_wait_rule: Record passive wait separately for model generation, command execution, queued execution, and later external review. Passive wait remains visible in wall-time metrics.
human_intervention.unknown_observation_rule: Unknown human-minute, token, cost, or downstream observations are recorded as unknown and are never silently removed from their declared denominator.

metrics.definition: Success-oracle passes divided by every declared applicable trial, including failed, stopped, inconclusive, conflict, rework, and recovery attempts.
Later detected task-caused defect or regression divided by all declared applicable trials.
Required safety, gate, and evidence fields present and internally consistent divided by all required fields across applicable trials.
Active human minutes only; passive wait is reported separately.
End-to-end elapsed time from trial start through required cleanup or stop receipt, reported with P50 and P95.
Capture available token usage and cost source; unavailable values remain unavailable rather than estimated as zero.
Count merge, patch, policy, or review conflicts and every rework loop per applicable trial.
Reproducible recovery or rollback successes divided by all declared recovery or rollback attempts.
Sampled human review disposition, merge/reject/rework, later CI, revert, or defect outcome. censored and unknown remain in the denominator.

generation_and_q0_rules.required_followup: A new trigger creates a new capability generation and requires the declared Q0 path before it can be selected by a profile. It is not a hot configuration switch.

decision_contract.preserve_rule: Only a non-normative preserve_v3_23_semantics decision releases P0A closure.
decision_contract.successor_rule: A decision that changes Batch prohibition, adapter, authority, concurrency, Q0 trigger, quality promotion, or truth source freezes v3.23 and requires a v3.24 successor candidate.

reference_basis.use: Compare representative task quality, evidence, latency, tokens, and cost rather than assuming the highest effort is optimal.
Treat App Server client integration as a distinct surface.
Treat managed worktree isolation and lifecycle as a distinct surface.
Treat unattended scheduling, sandbox, worktree, and effect behavior as a distinct surface.
